name: MCP Box Server
description: Box's official MCP server, a hosted remote at https://mcp.box.com using OAuth 2.0 with admin-managed enablement (Admin Console > Integrations) - the only supported path, as the self-hosted community Python server is deprecated. Tools cover user info, file/folder operations (read, list, search), and Box AI (Q&A across files, metadata extraction) over enterprise content. Mostly read plus AI extraction; permission-scoped to the authorizing user.

evidence.source: Box Developer Docs - Box MCP server
evidence.value: File and folder read, list, and content operations run on Box's mature content API platform

file_operation_reliability.methodology: Operation success rate assessment against the Box Platform API

evidence.value: Search tools build on Box's full-text and metadata search across the user's accessible content
Box AI tools provide Q&A across files and structured metadata extraction; quality depends on document type and the underlying Box AI models
Subject to Box per-user API rate limits; Box AI operations have separate capacity constraints

evidence.source: Box Developer Docs - Box MCP server
evidence.value: Standard Box API errors (permission, not-found, rate limit) are surfaced to the MCP client
OAuth 2.0 with Bearer tokens; client credentials are issued through Box's Admin Console or Developer Console and each user authorizes access individually

evidence.source: Box Support - Managing Box MCP Servers
evidence.value: MCP access is admin-managed: enterprise admins enable/configure the MCP server from Admin Console > Integrations, create Integration Credentials, and configure scopes such as Content Actions

evidence.source: Box Developer Docs - Box MCP server
evidence.value: The server's core function is feeding enterprise document content and Box AI answers over that content into agent contexts; any file a user can access (including externally shared or collaborator-uploaded documents) is an untrusted-content channel that can carry injected instructions

content_prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Analysis of document content as a prompt-injection vector into multi-tool agent contexts

evidence.value: Documented tool categories are user info, file/folder read-list-search operations, and Box AI Q&A/extraction - a mostly read-plus-AI surface with limited destructive write capability

data_modification_risk.methodology: Write-path and blast-radius assessment of the documented tool set

evidence.value: Content access via MCP is attributable to the authorizing user in Box enterprise event streams and admin reports
Enterprise document content - contracts, financials, HR files - retrieved or summarized via MCP tools enters the connected LLM provider's context

file_content_exposure.methodology: Data flow analysis of content exposure to the model context

evidence.value: Box AI metadata extraction pulls structured fields (names, dates, amounts) out of documents at scale, concentrating sensitive values into agent-readable form

metadata_extraction_privacy.methodology: Assessment of structured extraction over sensitive documents

evidence.source: Box Support - Managing Box MCP Servers
evidence.value: Access is permission-scoped to the authorizing user and governed by admin-configured scopes; Box's existing enterprise governance (permissions, classifications) applies to what MCP can reach
Content retrieved via MCP is processed by the connected LLM provider (Claude, Copilot Studio, ChatGPT, Le Chat) under that provider's privacy policy

evidence.source: Box Developer Docs - Box MCP server
evidence.value: First-party developer guide plus a public GitHub README covering OAuth setup, Admin Console configuration, and per-platform connection examples (Claude, Copilot Studio, Cursor, GitHub Copilot)
The hosted service itself is closed source; the MIT-licensed repo documents it, and the open-source self-hosted alternative (box-community/mcp-server-box) is deprecated for new work
Admins can observe MCP-driven content access through Box's enterprise event and reporting surfaces
Tools are enumerated by functional category (user info, file/folder operations, Box AI), making the capability surface explicit

evidence.source: Box Support - Managing Box MCP Servers
evidence.value: End users cannot simply connect: an enterprise admin must first enable MCP in Admin Console > Integrations and issue Integration Credentials (client ID/secret) before OAuth connection to https://mcp.box.com
File operations track Box API latency; Box AI Q&A and extraction calls add model-inference latency
Hosted on Box's production infrastructure with public status transparency and enterprise uptime track record
Covers content read/list/search and Box AI; deliberately narrower than the full Box API (limited write, no admin operations)

evidence.source: Box Developer Docs - Box MCP server
evidence.value: Documented integrations across Copilot Studio, Claude/Claude Code, Mistral Le Chat, ChatGPT, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and other MCP clients; also listed as a Microsoft connector and on AWS Marketplace

strengths: Official Box-hosted remote at https://mcp.box.com with OAuth 2.0 - the single supported path, replacing the deprecated self-hosted Python server
Admin-managed enablement: enterprise admins control activation, credentials, and scopes from Admin Console > Integrations
Mostly read-plus-AI tool surface (user info, file/folder read/list/search, Box AI Q&A and metadata extraction) - limited destructive blast radius
Access is permission-scoped to the authorizing user under Box's existing enterprise governance
Broad documented client support: Copilot Studio, Claude, ChatGPT, Mistral Le Chat, Cursor, GitHub Copilot
MCP-driven access attributable to the user in Box enterprise event streams
limitations: Prompt-injection-via-content territory: enterprise documents (including externally shared files) flow into agent contexts, so hostile content in any accessible file can carry instructions to a multi-tool agent
Enterprise file content and Box AI extractions are processed by the connected LLM provider
Admin enablement is a prerequisite - individual users cannot self-serve, and setup requires Integration Credentials plus scope configuration
Closed-source hosted service; the deprecated open-source self-hosted server is the only auditable variant
Narrower than the full Box API: limited write operations and no admin/governance tooling
Box AI extraction concentrates sensitive structured values (names, amounts, dates) into agent-readable output
Box AI and per-user API rate limits constrain heavy workloads

metadata.license: Proprietary (hosted service); documentation repo MIT; deprecated self-hosted server open source
supported_platforms: Any MCP client with HTTP transport and OAuth support
metadata.authentication: OAuth 2.0 with admin-issued Integration Credentials (client ID/secret); per-user authorization
metadata.self_hosted_status: Deprecated - box-community/mcp-server-box (Python) no longer recommended for new work
metadata.status: Active - hosted remote is the supported path

code-generation.notes: Marginal fit; useful mainly for pulling specs and docs stored in Box into coding agents

customer-support.notes: Good for grounding answers in policy and product documents stored in Box

content-creation.notes: Strong for drafting from existing enterprise documents and asset libraries

data-analysis.notes: Box AI metadata extraction turns document sets into structured data for analysis

research-assistant.notes: Excellent for Q&A and synthesis across large enterprise document repositories

legal-compliance.notes: Strong admin governance, but contract content reaching the LLM provider needs review against matter confidentiality

healthcare.notes: PHI in stored documents routed through agent contexts is high risk despite permission scoping

financial-analysis.notes: Good for extracting figures from financial documents; exposure of sensitive numbers to the LLM applies

education.notes: Good for course material Q&A and document-based learning workflows

creative-writing.notes: Limited fit beyond referencing source material stored in Box

best_for: Enterprises wanting governed AI access to Box content without exporting files
Document Q&A and synthesis across large repositories via Box AI
Metadata extraction workflows turning contracts and forms into structured data
Admin-controlled rollouts where IT must gate which users and scopes agents get
